target.name: Blackstar
target.founder: Daniel Edrisian (Founder/CEO, ex-OpenAI Codex engineer; previously founded Alex YC W24, an Xcode AI coding assistant acqui-hired by OpenAI Sept 2025; UC Berkeley EECS)

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $12M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $8.3B - $25.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Blackstar is a seed-stage AI & Software company founded by Daniel Edrisian, former OpenAI Codex engineer whose prior venture Alex (YC W24) was acqui-hired by OpenAI in September 2025. The company has raised $12M to date. The relevant comp universe comprises 3 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private peers (Reflection AI at $25.0B Series A, Applied Intuition at $15.0B Series A, Together AI at $8.3B Series A), alongside a broader set of 8 private comparables and 1 public reference (Palantir).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a proxy for current value and applying a typical 2.5x Seed-to-next-stage step-up multiplier, implies $30M. Peer-median analysis across the 3 identified comparables yielded a median valuation of $15.0B, with a range of $8.3B to $25.0B. The divergence between these methods reflects the gap between early-stage funding dynamics and late-stage peer multiples.

Triangulation across both methods produces a range of $26M (low) to $17.2B (high), with a median of $7.5B. The substantial width of this range reflects genuine valuation uncertainty inherent in seed-stage frontier tech. Key sensitivities include the strength of founder pedigree relative to product traction (currently undisclosed) and the applicability of Series A multiples to a seed-stage company. Resolution of these unknowns would materially narrow the range.
